tabs.label: Projects
tabs.content:
Working with Projects

Projects are the top-level container for all your work. Each project has its own board, backlog, timeline, and settings.

Creating a Project

  1. Click New Project from the sidebar
  2. Choose a template or start from scratch
  3. Configure your workflow stages (e.g., To Do, In Progress, Review, Done)
  4. Invite team members and set permissions

Project Templates

Templates save your workflow configuration, custom fields, and automations so you can reuse them. We provide built-in templates for Scrum, Kanban, Bug Tracking, and Product Launch.

tabs.label: Tasks
tabs.content:
Managing Tasks

Tasks are the atomic unit of work in FlowBoard. Each task belongs to a project and moves through your workflow stages.

Task Fields

  • Title — A clear, actionable description
  • Assignee — Who's responsible for this task
  • Priority — Critical, High, Medium, Low
  • Labels — Custom tags for filtering and grouping
  • Due Date — Deadline with optional time
  • Story Points — Effort estimation for sprint planning

Subtasks & Checklists

Break down complex tasks into subtasks (which appear on the board) or checklists (which live inside the task detail view).

tabs.label: Automations
tabs.content:
Automation Rules

Automations eliminate repetitive work by executing actions when specific conditions are met.

Anatomy of a Rule

Every automation has three parts:

  1. Trigger — The event that starts the rule (e.g., task moved to Review)
  2. Condition — Optional filter (e.g., only if priority is Critical)
  3. Action — What happens (e.g., assign to QA lead, post to Slack)

Common Recipes

  • Auto-assign tasks based on label
  • Send Slack notification when a blocker is created
  • Move task to Done when all subtasks complete
  • Set due date to 5 business days after creation

fields.code:
# Install the FlowBoard CLI
npm install -g @flowboard/cli

# Authenticate with your API key
flowboard auth login --key YOUR_API_KEY

# List all projects
flowboard projects list

# Create a new task
flowboard tasks create \
  --project "web-app" \
  --title "Implement user onboarding flow" \
  --assignee "jordan@apexdigital.com" \
  --priority high \
  --labels "feature,frontend"
fields.caption: Getting started with the FlowBoard CLI

items.title: How do I reset my API key?
items.content: Go to Settings → API Keys and click Regenerate next to the key you want to reset. Note that this immediately invalidates the old key — update your integrations before regenerating.

items.title: Can I import data from Jira?
items.content: Yes. Use our built-in migration tool at Settings → Import. We support Jira Cloud and Jira Server (7.x+). The migration preserves projects, tasks, comments, attachments, and custom fields. Typical migration takes 5-15 minutes depending on volume.

items.title: What are the API rate limits?
items.content:
Rate limits depend on your plan:

  • Starter: 1,000 requests/hour
  • Pro: 10,000 requests/hour
  • Enterprise: Unlimited (fair use policy)

Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining) are included in every API response.

items.title: How do webhooks work?
items.content: Webhooks send real-time HTTP POST notifications to your endpoint when events occur in FlowBoard. Supported events include task creation, status changes, comments, and sprint completions. Configure webhooks at Settings → Webhooks. Each webhook includes an HMAC signature for verification.

items.title: Is there a sandbox environment?
items.content: Yes. Every account includes a free sandbox workspace that mirrors your production setup. Use it for testing integrations, automation rules, and API calls without affecting live data. Access it at Settings → Sandbox.

fields.message: Need help? Our support team is available Monday – Friday, 9 AM – 6 PM PST. Average response time: under 2 hours.
